title: Advanced features
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived)

title: Advanced features
text: Pearson residuals measure much cell deviates independence assumption. Positive residuals indicate observations expected; negative residuals indicate fewer. Residuals automatically computed exposed .residuals computed variable, can map aesthetic via after_stat():  can also map residuals colour instead relying fill:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(       fill = Survived, weight = Freq,       alpha = after_stat(abs(.residuals))     ),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.3, 1), guide = "none") +   labs(title = "Residual shading: stronger opacity = larger deviation") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(     label = after_stat(round(.residuals, 1))   ), colour = "white", size = 3) +   labs(title = "Pearson residuals as labels")

title: Advanced features
text: geom_marimekko() supports multi-variable formulas. three-variable formula (~ X | Y | Z) partitions plot alternating directions (horizontal, vertical, horizontal): First split: horizontal X (column widths proportional X) Second split: vertical Y within column Third split: horizontal Z within cell  produces richer view faceting three variables share single coordinate space, making relative proportions directly comparable.
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived | Sex   ) +   labs(title = "Nested mosaic: Class > Sex > Survived")

title: Advanced features
text: default geom_marimekko() automatically labels axes category names. y-axis shows proportions 0 1, x-axis displays category labels column’s midpoint.

title: Advanced features
text: fortify_marimekko() returns computed tile positions plain data frame without creating plot. accepts formula syntax geom_marimekko(): Multi-variable formulas work : returned columns :

title: Advanced features
text: companion layers geom_marimekko_text(), geom_marimekko_label() automatically read tile positions preceding geom_marimekko() layer. need specify label aesthetic:  control, use fortify_marimekko() pre-compute tiles pass data standard ggplot2 geom. lets summarize, filter, transform tile data plotting:  fortify_marimekko() returns plain data frame, can use ggplot2 geom – geom_segment(), geom_curve(), geom_tile(), ggrepel::geom_label_repel(), etc.
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(     label = after_stat(paste(Class, Survived, weight, sep = "\n"))   ), colour = "white", size = 2.5) tiles <- fortify_marimekko(titanic,   formula = ~ Class | Survived, weight = Freq )  # Highlight cells with significant residuals tiles$significant <- abs(tiles$.residuals) > 2  ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_label(     data = tiles[tiles$significant, ],     aes(x = x, y = y, label = paste0("r=", round(.residuals, 1))),     fill = "yellow", size = 3, fontface = "bold"   ) +   labs(title = "Significant deviations from independence (|r| > 2)")

title: Advanced features
text: exported StatMarimekkoTiles ggproto object lets pair marimekko tile positions geom. convenience wrappers geom_marimekko_text() geom_marimekko_label() cover common case (text overlays), StatMarimekkoTiles gives full control plugging directly ggplot2::layer().

title: Advanced features
text: StatMarimekkoTiles compute tile positions — reads preceding geom_marimekko() layer via internal shared environment. means: geom_marimekko() layer must appear layer uses StatMarimekkoTiles. stat returns one row per tile columns xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, x, y (centre), weight, fill, .proportion, .residuals, .tooltip. can reference columns aes() via after_stat().

title: Advanced features
text: Map point size weight show tile counts bubbles:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, alpha = 0.4   ) +   layer(     stat = StatMarimekkoTiles,     geom = GeomPoint,     mapping = aes(size = after_stat(weight)),     data = titanic,     position = "identity",     show.legend = FALSE,     inherit.aes = FALSE,     params = list(colour = "white", alpha = 0.7)   ) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 12) +   labs(title = "Bubble overlay via StatMarimekkoTiles")

title: Advanced features
text: Colour size encode deviation independence:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   layer(     stat = StatMarimekkoTiles,     geom = GeomPoint,     mapping = aes(       size = after_stat(abs(.residuals)),       colour = after_stat(ifelse(.residuals > 0, "over", "under"))     ),     data = titanic,     position = "identity",     show.legend = TRUE,     inherit.aes = FALSE,     params = list(alpha = 0.8)   ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(over = "tomato", under = "steelblue"),     name = "Deviation"   ) +   scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 8), name = "|Residual|") +   labs(title = "Residual markers via StatMarimekkoTiles")

title: Advanced features
text: Use GeomRect draw highlighted borders around specific tiles (e.g. tiles large residuals):
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   layer(     stat = StatMarimekkoTiles,     geom = GeomRect,     mapping = aes(       linewidth = after_stat(ifelse(abs(.residuals) > 2, 1.5, 0))     ),     data = titanic,     position = "identity",     show.legend = FALSE,     inherit.aes = FALSE,     params = list(colour = "red", fill = NA)   ) +   labs(title = "Highlight tiles with |residual| > 2")

title: Advanced features
text: give access computed tile data, serve different purposes: Use StatMarimekkoTiles want add layers stay sync geom_marimekko() parameters. Use fortify_marimekko() need transform subset tile data passing geom.

title: Advanced features
text: marimekko produces standard ggplot2 layers, can freely combine multiple features:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(       fill = Survived, weight = Freq,       alpha = after_stat(abs(.residuals))     ),     formula = ~ Class | Survived,     show_percentages = TRUE   ) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(label = after_stat(weight)),     colour = "white", size = 3.5   ) +   scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.4, 1), guide = "none") +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(     title = "Full-featured mosaic plot",     subtitle = "Residual shading + counts + marginal %"   )

title: Advanced features
text: default, gap controls horizontal (columns) vertical (segments) spacing. Use gap_x gap_y set independently:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, gap_x = 0.04, gap_y = 0   ) +   labs(title = "Wide column gaps, no vertical gaps") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, gap_x = 0, gap_y = 0.03   ) +   labs(title = "No column gaps, visible vertical gaps")

title: Advanced features
text: marimekko ships Marimekko inspired color pallette. Use theme_marimekko() oe use scale_fill_manual(palette = marimekko_pal):  default, tile borders match fill colour (borders blend ). Set colour explicitly restore visible borders:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Earthy Nordic palette") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, colour = "white"   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "White borders with marimekko palette")

title: Advanced features
text: marimekko plots work plotly::ggplotly() box:
code: library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) ggplotly(p)

title: Advanced features
text: Unlike mosaic packages, marimekko supports arbitrary R expressions — formulas inside aes():
code: # Expressions work in formulas ggplot(mtcars) +   geom_marimekko(formula = ~ factor(cyl) | factor(gear)) +   labs(     y = "Gears", fill = "Gears",     title = "factor() inside formula works"   )

title: Advanced features
text: marimekko works correctly called :: notation (e.g., marimekko::geom_marimekko()) without requiring library(marimekko). makes safe use inside packages via Imports rather Depends.

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: marimekko (mosaic) plot two-dimensional visualization contingency table. column represents category one variable, segments within column represent categories second variable: - Column widths proportional marginal counts x variable. - Segment heights within column proportional conditional counts fill variable given x. marimekko package provides native ggplot2 layer, can combine ggplot2 functionality (facets, themes, annotations, etc.).

title: Getting started with marimekko
code: # From CRAN install.packages("marimekko")  # From GitHub (when published) devtools::install_github("gogonzo/marimekko")

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: built-Titanic dataset records survival counts class, sex, age. Let’s visualize survival passenger class.  Two components work: geom_marimekko() computes tile positions data. formula defines variables (columns segments), fill defines segment colours, weight provides counts. Axis labels automatically added. Standard ggplot2 functions (labs(), theme(), etc.) work usual.
code: library(ggplot2) library(marimekko)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic)  ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   labs(title = "Titanic survival by class")

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: geom_marimekko() understands aesthetics parameters: data already one row per observation (aggregation needed), omit weight:
code: ggplot(mtcars) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = factor(gear)),     formula = ~ cyl | gear   )

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: gap parameter controls spacing tiles fraction plot area. Default 0.01.  Set gap = 0 seamless mosaic:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, gap = 0.03   ) +   labs(title = "Wider gaps (gap = 0.03)") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, gap = 0   ) +   labs(title = "No gaps")

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: geom_marimekko() can append marginal percentages x-axis labels via show_percentages parameter:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived,     show_percentages = TRUE   )

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: Use geom_marimekko_text() (geom_marimekko_label() boxed version) place labels tile centers. Tile positions read automatically preceding geom_marimekko() layer — label aesthetic needed. Reference computed variables via after_stat(): weight – aggregated count tile cond_prop / .proportion – conditional proportion within parent .residuals – Pearson residual Original variable columns (e.g. Class, Survived)  Percentage labels:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(label = after_stat(weight)), colour = "white") +   labs(title = "Counts inside tiles") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(     label = after_stat(paste0(round(cond_prop * 100), "%"))   ), colour = "white", size = 3)

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: theme_marimekko() provides clean, minimal theme removes distracting x-axis gridlines:  Since builds theme_minimal(), can override element:
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "With theme_marimekko()") ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   theme_marimekko() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: geom_marimekko() supports ggplot2 faceting. panel gets independently proportioned mosaic:
code: ggplot(as.data.frame(Titanic)) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq), formula = ~ Class | Survived) +   facet_wrap(~Sex) +   labs(title = "Survival by class, faceted by sex")

title: Getting started with marimekko
text: See vignette("advanced-features") spine plots, Pearson residuals, three-variable mosaics, programmatic data extraction fortify_marimekko().

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic archived CRAN 2025-11-10 due uncorrected issues. Key problems included reliance internal ggplot2 APIs broke updates dependency deprecated tidyr functions. marimekko -scratch replacement uses public ggplot2 APIs, depends ggplot2 rlang, follows standard ggplot2 aesthetic conventions. examples adapted Jeppson & Hofmann (2023), showing ggmosaic pattern marimekko equivalent.

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic paper (Jeppson & Hofmann, 2023, R Journal 14(4)) uses fly dataset ggmosaic built-Titanic dataset. Since ggmosaic longer CRAN, recreate fly-based examples using Titanic HairEyeColor — built-R datasets need extra packages.

what: One dimensional — spine plot and bar chart
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper starts single-variable plots showing different divider types (Figure 1 Jeppson & Hofmann, 2023):   main difference: product() wrapper — use formula = ~ Var instead. marimekko divider argument; always produces vertical columns horizontal stacking.
code: # ggmosaic — spine plot (one variable, default divider) ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Class), fill = Class, weight = Freq)) # marimekko — one variable ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Class, weight = Freq), formula = ~Class) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "One variable: f(Class)") # ggmosaic — bar chart (one variable, hbar divider) ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(     aes(x = product(Class), fill = Class, weight = Freq),     divider = "hbar"   ) # ggplot — barchart # todo: needs percentage y scale ggplot(titanic) +   geom_bar(aes(Class, weight = Freq, fill = Class)) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "One variable: f(Class)")

what: Two dimensional — mosaic plot and stacked bar chart
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper shows two-dimensional mosaic plots variable order product() controls partitioning hierarchy (Figure 2):
code: # ggmosaic — two variables ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Class),     fill = Survived,     weight = Freq   )) # marimekko — two variables via formula ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Two variables: f(Survived | Class) f(Class)")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper shows two-dimensional mosaic plots variable order product() controls partitioning hierarchy (Figure 3):
code: # ggmosaic — double-decker variables ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Survived, Class, Sex),     fill = Survived,     weight = Freq,   ), divider = ddecker()) # marimekko — three variables via formula ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Sex + Class | Survived   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Three variables: f(Survived | Class, Sex)")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper demonstrates three-variable mosaics third variable adds another level partitioning (Figure 4):
code: # ggmosaic — three variables via product() ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Sex, Survived, Class),     fill = Survived,     weight = Freq   )) # marimekko — three variables via formula ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived | Sex   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Three variables: Class / Sex / Survived")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper shows conds creates conditional distributions (Section 3.2): marimekko conds aesthetic. Use facet_wrap() instead:
code: # ggmosaic — conditioning ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Class),     fill = Survived,     weight = Freq,     conds = product(Sex)   )) # marimekko — conditioning via faceting ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   facet_wrap(~Sex) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "f(Survived | Class) conditioned on Sex")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper demonstrates facet_grid() alternative conditioning (Section 3.3):
code: # ggmosaic — faceting ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq   )) +   facet_grid(~Sex) # marimekko — faceting works the same way ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   facet_wrap(~Sex) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Mosaic faceted by Sex")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper describes four fundamental partition types: hspine, vspine, hbar, vbar (Section 4, Figure 5). combined produce spine plots, stacked bar charts, mosaic plots, double decker plots. marimekko simplifies — geom_marimekko() always starts horizontal splits alternates direction | formula. Use coord_flip() need vertical-first orientation. equal-width columns (spine-plot style), standardize = TRUE available geom_marimekko_text() fortify_marimekko().
code: # ggmosaic — spine plot via divider ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     divider = c("vspine", "hspine")   ) # marimekko — proportional-width columns (default) ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Mosaic plot (proportional-width columns)")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper shows offset controls gaps tiles (Section 5): marimekko provides independent gap_x gap_y parameters:
code: # ggmosaic — single offset parameter ggplot(data = titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(     x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq   ), offset = 0.02) # marimekko — no gaps ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, gap_x = 0.02, gap_y = 0.02   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "No gaps")

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper shows geom_mosaic_text() adding labels (Section 7):  geom_marimekko_text() reads tile positions preceding geom_marimekko() layer — label aesthetic needed. Use after_stat() computed variables like weight (count), .proportion, original variable columns.
code: # ggmosaic — automatic text labels ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   geom_mosaic_text(aes(x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) # marimekko — automatic tile positions, only label needed ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(     label = after_stat(paste(Class, Survived, sep = "\n"))   )) +   theme_marimekko()

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper uses scale_x_productlist() axis labels:
code: # ggmosaic ggplot(titanic) +   geom_mosaic(aes(x = product(Class), fill = Survived, weight = Freq)) +   scale_x_productlist() # marimekko — with optional marginal percentages ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived,     show_percentages = TRUE   ) +   theme_marimekko()

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: paper uses multi-variable examples show mosaic plots reveal associations. use built-HairEyeColor dataset:  Three-variable version Sex:
code: ggplot(hair) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Eye, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Hair | Eye,     show_percentages = TRUE   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Hair color vs Eye color") ggplot(hair) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Eye, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Hair | Sex | Eye   ) +   theme_marimekko() +   labs(title = "Hair / Sex / Eye")

previous_headings: Key differences to be aware of
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic required wrapping variables product(). marimekko uses formula-based API: formula = ~ | b — specifies variable hierarchy (| alternates direction, + groups) fill — tile colour (defaults last formula variable) weight — observation weights / counts

previous_headings: Key differences to be aware of
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic handled axis labels internally. marimekko also adds axis labels automatically via geom_marimekko(). Use show_percentages = TRUE geom_marimekko() append marginal percentages x-axis labels.

previous_headings: Key differences to be aware of
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic used divider = c("vspine", "hspine", ...) control partitioning direction. marimekko encodes direction formula — | alternates horizontal vertical, always starting horizontal. Use coord_flip() vertical-first orientation.

previous_headings: Key differences to be aware of
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: ggmosaic set inherit.aes = FALSE default, requiring repeat aesthetics every layer. marimekko follows ggplot2 convention — inherit.aes = TRUE — aesthetics set ggplot(aes(...)) inherited.

previous_headings: Key differences to be aware of
what: 5. Text labels need explicit label aesthetic
title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: geom_marimekko_text() requires label = after_stat(weight) similar. ggmosaic’s geom_mosaic_text() auto-generated labels.

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: marimekko plots work plotly::ggplotly() box — simply pass plot object get interactive version: also works geom_marimekko_text(), geom_marimekko_label()
code: library(plotly)  p <- ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   )  ggplotly(p)

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: quick mechanical migration, apply replacements: manually: Remove product() wrappers – add formula = ~ Var1 | Var2 instead Remove conds aesthetic – use facet_wrap() multi-variable formula Remove scale_x_productlist() / scale_y_productlist() – axis labels now automatic Remove divider = ... – direction encoded formula Replace offset = ... gap / gap_x / gap_y Add explicit label = after_stat(weight) text layers Set inherit.aes = FALSE deliberately needed Move show_percentages = TRUE scale geom_marimekko(..., show_percentages = TRUE)
code: geom_mosaic(          →  geom_marimekko( geom_mosaic_text(     →  geom_marimekko_text( theme_mosaic(         →  theme_marimekko( library(ggmosaic)     →  library(marimekko)

title: Migrating from ggmosaic to marimekko
text: Jeppson, H. Hofmann, H. (2023). Generalized Mosaic Plots ggplot2 Framework. R Journal, 14(4), 50–73. doi:10.32614/RJ-2023-013

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dawid Kałędkowski. Author, maintainer.           ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9533-457X>

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kaledkowski D (2026). marimekko: Marimekko Mosaic Plots 'ggplot2'. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/gogonzo/marimekko.
code: @Manual{,   title = {marimekko: Marimekko and Mosaic Plots for 'ggplot2'},   author = {Dawid Kaledkowski},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/gogonzo/marimekko}, }

what: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: marimekko (mosaic) plots ggplot2. one-sided formula controls variable hierarchy split directions. Column widths segment heights encode marginal conditional proportions categorical variables.

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("marimekko")  # Or install the development version from GitHub devtools::install_github("gogonzo/marimekko")

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: library(ggplot2) library(marimekko)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic)  ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   labs(title = "Titanic survival by class", y = "Proportion") +   theme_marimekko()

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: one-sided formula (~ ...) controls variables used, nesting order, level splits plot area. plot starts single rectangle (unit square) variable subdivides .

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: two split directions: Horizontal split — divides area side--side columns along x-axis. Column widths proportional variable’s distribution. columns share vertical extent. Vertical split — divides area stacked rows along y-axis. Row heights proportional variable’s distribution. rows share horizontal extent. first variable always splits horizontally (columns). | switches direction, second variable splits vertically (rows within column), third switches back horizontal, .

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: Variables formula can arbitrary R expressions:
code: ~ factor(cyl) | cut(mpg, breaks = 3)

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived,     show_percentages = TRUE   ) +   theme_marimekko()

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
code: ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_marimekko_text(aes(label = after_stat(weight)))

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: marimekko extends ggplot2 ggproto system: StatMarimekko parses formula, recursively partitions unit square, returns tile rectangles (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) computed variables (.residuals, .proportion, .marginal). Tiles rendered via GeomRect sensible defaults (white borders, slight transparency). Axis labels automatically placed geom tile midpoints.

title: marimekko: marimekko and Mosaic Plots for ggplot2
text: marimekko designed avoid pain points existing packages. Minimal dependencies – ggplot2 dependency internal ggplot2 API usage – won’t break ggplot2 updates Easily extendable – StatMarimekkoTiles exposes tile data can pair ggplot2 geom build custom companion layers (bubbles, residual markers, etc.) Formula-based API – ~ | b | c encodes variables directions Works without library() – marimekko::geom_marimekko() just works Respects factor levels – user-set levels() honored -formula expressions – ~ factor(cyl) | cut(mpg, breaks = 3) works Plotly compatible – ggplotly() works box

what: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
title: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
text: Retrieve computed tile positions marimekko layer

title: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
code: StatMarimekkoTiles

title: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
text: object class StatMarimekkoTiles (inherits Stat, ggproto, gg) length 2.

title: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
text: Use StatMarimekkoTiles stat argument ggplot2::layer() pair tile data geom. requirement geom_marimekko() must appear custom layer tile positions computed first.

title: Retrieve computed tile positions from a marimekko layer — StatMarimekkoTiles
code: library(ggplot2)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic)  # Bubble overlay — point size encodes tile count ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived, alpha = 0.4   ) +   layer(     stat = StatMarimekkoTiles,     geom = GeomPoint,     mapping = aes(size = after_stat(weight)),     data = titanic,     position = "identity",     show.legend = FALSE,     inherit.aes = FALSE,     params = list(colour = "white", alpha = 0.7)   ) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 12)   # Residual markers — colour and size show deviation from independence ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   layer(     stat = StatMarimekkoTiles,     geom = GeomPoint,     mapping = aes(       size = after_stat(abs(.residuals)),       colour = after_stat(ifelse(.residuals > 0, "over", "under"))     ),     data = titanic,     position = "identity",     show.legend = TRUE,     inherit.aes = FALSE,     params = list(alpha = 0.8)   ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c(over = "tomato", under = "steelblue"),     name = "Deviation"   ) +   scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 8), name = "|Residual|")

what: Compute marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_marimekko

title: Compute marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_marimekko
code: fortify_marimekko(   data,   formula,   weight = NULL,   gap = 0.01,   gap_x = NULL,   gap_y = NULL,   standardize = FALSE )

title: Compute marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_marimekko
text: data data frame. formula one-sided formula specifying mosaic hierarchy, using syntax geom_marimekko(). Example: ~ Class | Survived. weight Name weight variable (unquoted string), NULL unweighted counts. Default NULL. gap Numeric. Size gap tiles. Default 0.01. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap. Overrides gap x. Default NULL. gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap. Overrides gap y. Default NULL. standardize Logical. Equal-width columns. Default FALSE.

title: Compute marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_marimekko
text: data frame columns formula variable, plus fill, colour, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, x, y, weight, .proportion, .marginal, .residuals.

title: Compute marimekko tile rectangles as a data frame — fortify_marimekko

what: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: Generalized mosaic plot formula-based variable nesting

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
code: geom_marimekko(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   formula = NULL,   gap = 0.01,   gap_x = NULL,   gap_y = NULL,   colour = NULL,   alpha = 0.9,   show_percentages = FALSE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE,   ... )

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: mapping Aesthetic mapping. Optionally accepts fill weight pre-aggregated data. fill specified, defaults last variable formula. fill variable controls tile colour need appear formula. data data frame. formula one-sided formula specifying mosaic hierarchy. See sections detailed explanation. Quick reference: ~ | b — h(), v(b). Standard mosaic. ~ | b | c — h(), v(b), h(c). Alternating mosaic. ~ + b | c — h(), h(b), v(c). Double decker. ~ | b + c — h(), v(b), v(c). Multiple vertical variables. gap Numeric. Gap tiles fraction plot area. Default 0.01. gap_x Numeric. Horizontal gap override. Default NULL (uses gap). gap_y Numeric. Vertical gap override. Default NULL (uses gap). colour Tile border colour. Default NULL (border). Can also mapped via aes(colour = variable). alpha Tile transparency. Default 0.9. show_percentages Logical. TRUE, appends marginal percentage x-axis label. Default FALSE. na.rm Logical. Remove missing values. Default FALSE. show.legend Logical. Show legend. Default NA. inherit.aes Logical. Inherit aesthetics ggplot(). Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed layer.

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: list ggplot2 layers (geom + axis scales).

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: formula uses two operators encode full partitioning hierarchy single expression: | (pipe) Separates nesting levels. | switches splitting direction, alternating horizontal, vertical, horizontal, vertical, . first variable (group) listed outermost split — partitions entire plot area. subsequent level partitions tiles created previous level. + (plus) Groups variables nesting level. variables joined + share splitting direction applied sequentially within level. first + variable partitions current tiles, second + variable subdivides tiles, still direction.

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: formula read left right, coarsest (outermost) partition finest (innermost): ~ | b First split plot horizontally (columns whose widths reflect marginal proportions ). , within column, split vertically b (rows whose heights reflect conditional proportions b given ). classic two-variable marimekko / mosaic plot. ~ | b | c Horizontal , vertical b, horizontal c. Three levels nesting alternating directions (h \(\\) v \(\\) h). ~ + b | c Horizontal , horizontal b (direction + groups ), vertical c. double decker pattern — horizontal splits first, single vertical split end. ~ | b + c Horizontal , vertical b, vertical c. Two vertical variables nested within column.

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
text: stat computes following variables can accessed ggplot2::after_stat(): .proportion Conditional proportion tile within immediate parent. formula ~ | b, proportion b within level , .e. \(P(b \mid )\). Values sum 1 within parent tile. Useful mapping alpha fade tiles local share: aes(alpha = after_stat(.proportion)). .marginal Joint (marginal) proportion tile relative whole dataset, .e. \(n_\text{cell} / N\). Values sum 1 across tiles. Used internally x-axis percentage labels show_percentages = TRUE, can mapped aesthetics emphasise cells overall frequency. .residuals Pearson residual measuring departure statistical independence horizontal vertical variable groups. Computed \((O - E) / \sqrt{E}\), \(O\) observed cell count \(E\) count expected independence. Positive values indicate cell frequent expected; negative values indicate less frequent. one direction (horizontal vertical) present, .residuals set 0. Map alpha fill highlight deviations: aes(alpha = after_stat(abs(.residuals))).

title: Generalized mosaic plot with formula-based variable nesting — geom_marimekko
code: library(ggplot2)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic)  # 2-variable mosaic ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   )   # 3-variable mosaic (h -> v -> h) ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived | Sex   )   # Multi-variable fill with interaction() ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = interaction(Sex, Survived), weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Sex + Survived   )   # Fade tiles by conditional proportion ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, alpha = after_stat(.proportion), weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   guides(alpha = "none")   # Highlight cells that deviate from independence ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, alpha = after_stat(abs(.residuals)), weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   guides(alpha = "none")

code: geom_marimekko_label(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   position = "identity",   ...,   size = 3.5,   colour = "black",   fill = alpha("white", 0.7),   label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = FALSE,   inherit.aes = FALSE )

title: Add labels with background to a marimekko plot — geom_marimekko_label
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings. label required. Use ggplot2::after_stat() computed variables. data data frame. Default NULL (uses plot data; tile positions come geom_marimekko()). position Position adjustment. Default "identity". ... Additional arguments passed layer. size Text size. Default 3.5. colour Text colour. Default "white" text, "black" labels. fill Label background colour. Default alpha("white", 0.7). label.padding Amount padding around label. Default ggplot2::unit(0.15, "lines"). na.rm Logical. Remove missing values. Default FALSE. show.legend Logical. Show legend. Default FALSE. inherit.aes Logical. Inherit aesthetics. Default FALSE.

title: Add labels with background to a marimekko plot — geom_marimekko_label
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Add labels with background to a marimekko plot — geom_marimekko_label
code: library(ggplot2)  titanic <- as.data.frame(Titanic) ggplot(titanic) +   geom_marimekko(     aes(fill = Survived, weight = Freq),     formula = ~ Class | Survived   ) +   geom_marimekko_label(aes(label = after_stat(weight)))

title: marimekko: Marimekko Plots for 'ggplot2' — marimekko-package
text: Create marimekko (mosaic) plots 'ggplot2' layer. Column widths encode marginal proportions one categorical variable segment heights encode conditional proportions second categorical variable. Based mosaic display method Hartigan Kleiner (1981) doi:10.1007/978-1-4613-9464-8_37 .

title: marimekko: Marimekko Plots for 'ggplot2' — marimekko-package
text: Maintainer: Dawid Kałędkowski dawid.kaledkowski@gmail.com (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9533-457X>)

title: Unikko-inspired colour palette — marimekko_pal
text: character vector 8 bold colours inspired Marimekko's iconic Unikko poppy pattern. Vibrant, high-contrast tones suited categorical data visualisation.

title: marimekko 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-16 Initial CRAN release.

title: marimekko 0.1.0
text: geom_marimekko() — formula-based mosaic/marimekko plots ggplot2 layer. One-sided formulas (~ | b) encode variable hierarchy split direction. | alternates horizontal vertical splits; + groups variables direction. geom_marimekko_text() geom_marimekko_label() — place text label annotations tile centres, access computed variables via after_stat(). fortify_marimekko() — extract tile rectangles data frame without plotting. theme_marimekko() — minimal theme designed mosaic plots. marimekko_pal — categorical colour palette.

title: marimekko 0.1.0
text: .proportion — conditional proportion within parent tile. .marginal — joint proportion relative whole dataset. .residuals — Pearson residuals measuring departure independence.

title: marimekko 0.1.0
text: show_percentages = TRUE appends marginal percentages x-axis labels. Independent gap control via gap_x gap_y. Formulas support arbitrary R expressions (e.g., ~ factor(cyl) | cut(mpg, 3)). Compatible facet_wrap(), facet_grid(), plotly::ggplotly().
